title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
At the Brawnsham Hotel restaurant, Luca Lazari introduces a new employee, Thomas Breenell. Breenell testifies that he encountered Richard Nigges near the dining room elevators at 7:30 p.m. Nigges expressed concern for Mrs. Westphal and Miss Gransbury, requesting that Breenell ensure their dinner bills were not charged to their personal accounts. Breenell notes that Nigges had received a meal in Room 317 only fifteen minutes prior, raising questions as to why Nigges would leave his food to handle these financial matters.

During a tense moment in the restaurant, Mr. Burbank questions Nigges's movements and motives. A woman named Theresa Studd becomes distressed by the scrutiny but is calmed by a young woman and a promise from the narrator that she may soon return to her children. Poirot then presents a white piece of paper found by John Good at 8:10 p.m., which contains a killer's note reading: "May they rest in peace, 121, 238, 317." Despite the note's appearance, no witnesses come forward.

Later, the narrator and Poirot retreat to a secret lounge behind the restaurant. Poirot expresses anxiety regarding news of a cufflink with the initials "PIJ" found in Jenny's mouth. They discuss inconsistencies in the murders, specifically regarding the tea service. While Raphael Bork had sent three teacups to Ada Gransbury's room, only two were found there; the third was discovered in Harriet Sippler's room with her body. Poirot also notes the anomaly of the sherry glass found in Nigges's room, which no staff member admits to delivering.
